data.title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs T-Mobile US Merger As Valuation Gap Widens
data.description:
• Deutsche Telekom (XTRA:DTE) is in early talks over a potential full merger with T-Mobile US through a new holding company.
  • The combined entity would target dual listings and could become the world’s largest telecom operator by market value.
  • If agreed, the transaction might rank as one of the largest public M&A deals on record, subject to regulatory and political review.

Investors are assessing this development against a mixed recent share price picture. Deutsche Telekom trades at €27.61, with the stock down 6.7% over the past week and 14.6% over the past month, while the 5 year return sits at 103.2%. That longer term performance shows how important T-Mobile US has been for the group’s equity story.

The proposed merger would go far beyond routine capital markets moves and could restructure how investors access Deutsche Telekom’s core US asset. Discussions are still at an early stage and subject to significant regulatory and geopolitical uncertainty, so readers may want to focus on how deal terms, governance and listing structure evolve before drawing strong conclusions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Deutsche Telekom by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Deutsche Telekom.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 5 things going right for Deutsche Telekom that every investor should see.

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At €27.61 versus a €38.28 consensus target, the share price sits about 28% below where analysts expect it to be.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are flagged as trading around 76.1% below an estimated fair value, which points to a large valuation gap.
  • ❌ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of about 14.6% decline shows sentiment has been weak into these merger headlines.

There is only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold Deutsche Telekom. Head to the Simply Wall St company report for the latest analysis of Deutsche Telekom's Fair Value.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 A full merger with T-Mobile US would concentrate even more of Deutsche Telekom's story in the US asset and could change how you think about group exposure.
  • 📊 Watch how the proposed holding company, dual listings, governance structure and any share exchange ratio are framed as talks progress.
  • ⚠️ The company already carries high debt, so investors should track how any deal is financed and what it means for leverage and credit quality.

Dig Deeper

data.title: Carlisle Companies Q1 Margin Pullback Tests Long Term Expansion Thesis
data.description:
Carlisle Companies (CSL) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$1,052.1 million and basic EPS of US$3.13, setting the tone for how the rest of the year might shape up for margin focused investors. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from US$1,095.8 million and EPS of US$3.16 in Q1 2025 to a peak of US$1,449.5 million with EPS of US$5.93 in Q2 2025, before stepping down to US$1,052.1 million and EPS of US$3.13 in the latest quarter as attention shifts firmly to how consistently Carlisle can protect its profitability.

See our full analysis for Carlisle Companies.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to line these numbers up against the most common narratives around Carlisle Companies to see which views are supported and which are being challenged by the current margin profile.

See what the community is saying about Carlisle Companies

Advertisement

Margins Ease Back From 16.6% To 14.6%

  • Over the trailing twelve months, net profit margin sits at 14.6%, compared with 16.6% a year earlier, alongside Q1 2026 net income of US$127.7 million on US$1,052.1 million of revenue.
  • Consensus narrative leans on long term margin expansion. However, the move from 16.6% to 14.6% and Q1 earnings of US$3.13 per share prompt a check on how quickly efficiency programs and premium product mix are feeding through to profitability.
    • The view that operational programs can add 200+ basis points of margin over time sits next to a trailing margin that is currently around 200 basis points lower than last year.
    • Claims that higher margin building envelope products support strong profitability are being tested by recent quarters where EPS moved from US$5.93 in Q2 2025 to just above US$3.10 in Q1 2026.

Five Year EPS Trend Versus Recent Cooling

  • Five year earnings growth averaged 13.1% per year, while the most recent twelve month period showed negative earnings growth relative to that multi year trend, with trailing EPS at US$17.38 versus higher levels in prior trailing snapshots.
  • Bulls highlight stable reroofing demand and policy support as foundations for continued EPS expansion. At the same time, the recent pullback from trailing EPS above US$18 to US$17.38 and quarterly swings from US$5.93 to US$3.13 show that execution and market conditions can weigh on that longer term growth story.
    • The consensus view that rerouting toward higher value insulation and roofing systems can support earnings growth meets a data set where trailing net income eased from US$863.4 million to US$728.6 million over the past few trailing snapshots.
    • Expectations of long term growth tied to operational efficiencies sit alongside the past year, where earnings growth did not match the 13.1% annual pace seen over five years.
Have a closer look at how the bullish thesis lines up with Carlisle Companies' multi year earnings record and recent margin moves in light of the latest numbers, then contrast that with how other investors frame the upside case with 🐂 Carlisle Companies Bull Case.

P/E Of 20.2x And 3.1% Gap To DCF Fair Value

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 20.2x versus a peer average of 59.1x and industry average of 22.1x, and the current price of US$360.07 sits about 3.1% below a DCF fair value of roughly US$371.49.
  • Bears point to margin pressure, elevated debt, and recent insider selling, yet the combination of a 14.6% trailing net margin and a price only slightly below DCF fair value suggests the cautious view is being weighed against valuation that is broadly in line with the provided fair value range.
    • The share price sits below the DCF fair value of US$371.49 and under the analyst price target of US$406.00 while margins are still in the mid teens, which differs from a pure downside story focused only on weaker profitability.
    • Concerns about insider selling and leverage are arriving in a context where earnings are still forecast to grow about 7.3% per year and revenue about 4.1% per year, according to the provided figures.

data.title: Electrolux (OM:ELUX B) Q4 Profitability Rebound Tests Bearish Margin Narratives
data.description:
AB Electrolux (OM:ELUX B) has kicked off Q1 2026 with investors focused on how its recent revenue base of about SEK35.1b in Q4 2025 and basic EPS of SEK1.72 translate into margin resilience and earnings quality. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from SEK37.97b in Q4 2024 to SEK35.11b in Q4 2025, while basic EPS shifted from SEK0.56 to SEK1.72 over the same period, framing a very different earnings picture on a slightly smaller top line. With the shares around SEK45.10 and trailing results shaped by both improving profitability and a sizeable one off gain, the latest numbers put margins and earnings durability firmly in the spotlight for investors.

See our full analysis for AB Electrolux.

Next up is how these fresh results line up against the dominant AB Electrolux narratives, highlighting where the numbers back the story and where they start to challenge it.

See what the community is saying about AB Electrolux

Advertisement

EPS Build Up Reaches SEK3.25 Over 12 Months

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, basic EPS has moved from a loss of SEK5.16 in Q4 2024 to a profit of SEK3.25 by Q4 2025, with net income excluding extra items at SEK878 million on SEK131.3b of revenue.
  • What stands out for the bullish view is that this return to profit lines up with expectations for roughly 13% yearly earnings growth, yet
    • a SEK673 million one off gain in the last 12 months means part of the SEK878 million profit is not from the underlying run rate, which makes it harder to point to a clean base for the bullish earnings ramp.
    • consensus narrative talks about rising margins over time, but the history of multi year earnings declines in the inputs shows that a single profitable year, especially one helped by a one time gain, does not fully settle that concern.

Bulls argue that a cleaner earnings profile could justify a stronger long term story. It is therefore worth seeing how this profit swing fits into the wider growth narrative before leaning too hard on headline EPS. 🐂 AB Electrolux Bull Case

Quarterly Net Income Swings Against Bearish Fears

  • Looking at the last six reported quarters, net income excluding extra items moved from a loss of SEK235 million in Q3 2024 to profits of SEK42 million, SEK178 million, SEK192 million and then SEK466 million by Q4 2025, alongside revenue in a SEK31.3b to SEK37.0b range.
  • Bears often focus on weak profitability and pressure on European demand, yet
    • the step up from small losses and near break even in early 2025 to SEK466 million of profit in Q4 2025 shows that cost efficiency programs and mix focus cited in the cautious narrative are already feeding into higher quarterly earnings than the pessimistic path would suggest.
    • the bearish scenario assumes margins will still need to climb from 0.4% to 3.0% over several years, but the recent sequence of positive quarters in the data implies the company is already operating above the loss making profile that more negative views worry about.

Valuation Gap Versus DCF And Price Target

  • With the share price at SEK45.10, the stock sits well below the provided DCF fair value of SEK208.94 and also under the allowed analyst price target of SEK69.00, while the trailing P/E of 13.9x is close to the 14x industry average and below the 49.7x peer average.
  • Consensus narrative highlights modest 1.4% yearly revenue growth and a margin move from 0.7% to 2.5%, and the current pricing throws up a few tensions
    • the large gap to DCF fair value and discount to the SEK69.00 target imply the market is treating risks like weak interest coverage and the one off gain very seriously, even though the company has moved back into profit on SEK131.3b of trailing revenue.
    • at the same time, a P/E that is in line with the wider consumer durables industry but below the higher peer average suggests investors are pricing in those financial pressures while still acknowledging the improving trailing EPS profile of SEK3.25.

Skeptics point out that interest payments are not well covered by earnings and the share price has been volatile recently, so anyone looking at the discount to DCF fair value needs to weigh that against balance sheet and earnings quality risks. 🐻 AB Electrolux Bear Case

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) After Strong Multi‑Year Share Gains?
data.description:
• Wondering if Cadence Design Systems at around US$332.89 is still a solid opportunity or already pricing in a lot of expectations? This article walks through how the current share price stacks up against different valuation checks.
  • The stock has recent returns of 7.0% over 7 days, 18.3% over 30 days, 7.2% year to date, 14.9% over 1 year, 58.9% over 3 years and 152.6% over 5 years, which raises fair questions about how much value is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent headlines around Cadence Design Systems have focused on its role within software and design tools, as investors continue to pay attention to how these kinds of businesses are being valued in the broader market. This context helps explain why the share price and sentiment around the company remain closely watched.
  • Despite this attention, Cadence Design Systems currently has a valuation score of 0 out of 6. The next sections will break down what traditional valuation methods say about the stock and then finish with a way to think about value that goes beyond just the standard ratios.

Cadence Design Systems scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Cadence Design Systems Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return.

For Cadence Design Systems, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.59b. Analyst estimates and subsequent extrapolations point to free cash flow of about $2.54b in 2028, with a series of annual projections extending out to 2035 that are discounted back to today.

Bringing all those projected cash flows back to present value produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $194.75 per share, compared with the current share price of roughly $332.89. This implies the stock is around 70.9% above the model’s estimate of fair value, so on this measure the shares screen as expensive rather than cheap.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Cadence Design Systems may be overvalued by 70.9%. Discover 56 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Cadence Design Systems.

Approach 2: Cadence Design Systems Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Cadence Design Systems, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. Investors typically accept a higher P/E when they expect stronger growth or see the business as relatively resilient, and look for a lower P/E when growth expectations are more modest or risks feel higher.

Cadence Design Systems currently trades on a P/E of about 82.88x. That sits well above the broader Software industry average of roughly 30.50x, and also above a peer group average of about 42.59x. On simple comparisons, the stock is priced at a premium to both its sector and peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Cadence Design Systems is 37.74x. This proprietary metric estimates what a more tailored P/E might look like after accounting for factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, risk characteristics, industry and overall size. Because it blends these elements, the Fair Ratio can provide a more company specific anchor than broad industry or peer averages alone.

Comparing the current P/E of 82.88x with the Fair Ratio of 37.74x suggests the shares are trading above what this framework would view as a more balanced level.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 19 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Cadence Design Systems Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to think about value than ratios alone. On Simply Wall St this shows up as Narratives, where you pair your view of Cadence Design Systems with specific assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins to arrive at your own fair value that can be compared to the current price.

A Narrative is essentially your story about the company, written in numbers. You link what you believe about Cadence Design Systems, such as whether it becomes an efficient compounder with higher margins or faces pressure from open source tools and geopolitics, to a forecast and then to a fair value estimate.

On the Simply Wall St Community page, Narratives are set up so you can pick or adjust forecasts rather than build a full model from scratch. The platform, which is already used by millions of investors, keeps the Fair Value output updated when fresh news, earnings or guidance are incorporated.

For Cadence Design Systems, one published Narrative currently points to a fair value of about US$311.75 while another points to about US$418.00. This shows how two investors can look at the same company, apply different assumptions, and then use the gap between their Fair Value and the live share price to decide whether they see it as more attractive or more fully priced.

For Cadence Design Systems, however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Cadence Design Systems Narratives:

🐂 Cadence Design Systems Bull Case

Fair value in this bullish Narrative: about US$371.68 per share.

Current price vs this fair value: roughly 10.5% below the Narrative fair value, based on the latest close of US$332.89.

Revenue growth used in this Narrative: 14.19% a year.

  • Analysts backing this view see AI focused design tools, partnerships with large chip players and a growing IP business as key supports for future revenue and earnings.
  • They build in rising profit margins and ongoing share buybacks, which together lift earnings per share in their model.
  • They arrive at a fair value of about US$371.68, which sits within a wider analyst price target range of US$275.00 to US$410.00.

🐻 Cadence Design Systems Bear Case

Fair value in this more cautious Narrative: about US$311.75 per share.

Current price vs this fair value: roughly 6.8% above the Narrative fair value, based on the latest close of US$332.89.

Revenue growth used in this Narrative: 14.25% a year.

  • The bearish Narrative focuses on risks around open source and low cost alternatives, tougher competition and customer concentration, which could pressure pricing and margins.
  • It also highlights exposure to export controls and China, as well as questions about how much investors might be willing to pay for earnings if software sector valuation multiples stay tighter.
  • Using slightly different margin and discount rate assumptions, this Narrative settles on a fair value of about US$311.75, closer to the lower end of the current analyst target range.

These two Cadence Design Systems Narratives sit on the same set of facts but use different assumptions about growth, margins, risks and the P/E investors might accept. Over the past year, the company has seen revenue move from US$79.3 million to US$91.1 million and basic EPS shift from US$1.39 to US$1.72. Net income for the same period went from US$20.7 million to US$25.6 million, providing a clear view of the recent earnings trajectory. With a 28.1% net margin over the last 12 months, the latest print highlights a business where profitability is central to how investors may interpret this quarter.

  • For readers taking a more bullish angle, one focus is that this larger loan book sits alongside trailing earnings growth of 23.9% and a 28.1% net margin, which together support the idea of a bank earning solid profits on a growing base of loans.

data.title: PACE Act Puts Circle’s Fed Access And Legal Risks In Focus
data.description:
• The proposed PACE Act could give Circle Internet Group direct access to Federal Reserve payment rails.
  • Such access would allow Circle to settle transactions without relying on traditional partner banks.
  • The development focuses on payment infrastructure rather than short term trading sentiment.

Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) sits at the intersection of digital assets and traditional payments, and the PACE Act targets exactly that junction. Shares most recently traded at $99.66, with a return of 19.4% year to date, while shorter term moves include a 5.9% decline over the past week and a 4.0% decline over the past month. For readers, the key point is that the news centers on potential access to core payment infrastructure, not on earnings guidance or analyst commentary.

If the PACE Act progresses, investors may focus on how direct Fed access could affect Circle's role in institutional payments and settlement. This development could influence practical considerations such as transaction speed, counterparty risk, and Circle's position among nonbank payment firms, helping readers frame CRCL's story beyond short term price moves.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Circle Internet Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Circle Internet Group.

Is Circle Internet Group's balance sheet strong enough for future acquisitions? Dive into our detailed financial health analysis.

The PACE Act sits against a complex backdrop for Circle, where regulatory access and legal scrutiny are moving in opposite directions. On one side, a path to direct Federal Reserve access could put Circle in a similar payments tier to large banks and nonbank operators such as Ripple. This could have implications for settlement speed, operating resilience, and dependence on partner banks. On the other side, Circle is facing a class action lawsuit tied to the Drift Protocol hack, where plaintiffs argue the company should have frozen an estimated US$230 million of stolen USDC that reportedly moved across its own bridge over several hours. For investors, the tension is clear: regulators weighing which nonbank issuers to let into core payment systems are also likely to look closely at incident response, controls, and past compliance history. The way these regulatory and legal processes develop over the next few years could influence the range of costs, restrictions, or operating conditions attached to any direct Fed access, rather than simply opening the door unconditionally.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Circle Internet Group Narrative

  • The PACE Act aligns with the narrative that regulators are formalizing frameworks for dollar stablecoins and tokenized payments. This could support Circle’s push to position USDC as core settlement infrastructure alongside major institutions.
  • The Drift Protocol lawsuit and references to earlier alleged compliance issues directly challenge the idea that increasing regulatory clarity automatically benefits Circle, because policymakers may weigh these incidents when deciding how much access and oversight to apply.
  • The specific question of how Circle uses its freezing powers on USDC and associated bridges during large exploits is not fully captured by high level themes about regulatory clarity and institutional adoption. However, it could influence both reputational risk and any conditions attached to Fed access.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Circle Internet Group to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The class action tied to the Drift Hack adds legal uncertainty, including the potential for financial penalties or mandated changes in controls, which could increase compliance costs and affect how Circle operates USDC in high risk situations.
  • ⚠️ If regulators or central banks view the lawsuit and prior alleged compliance failures as signs of weak incident management, Circle could face tighter supervision, slower progress on direct Fed access, or more restrictive terms than competitors such as Tether or Ripple.
  • 🎁 A clear statutory route into Federal Reserve payment rails would, if obtained, give Circle an alternative to partner banks for settlement. This could appeal to institutional users focused on counterparty risk and operational reliability.
  • 🎁 The legislative focus on nonbank payment providers supports the idea that stablecoin issuers can become part of mainstream payments. This may help Circle frame USDC as regulated infrastructure when speaking with banks, payment networks, and large platforms.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it may be useful to track how the PACE Act progresses through Congress, any changes to its provisions on “registered covered providers,” and whether Circle publicly signals interest in applying for that status. Developments in the Drift Protocol lawsuit are also important, including any settlements or court findings on Circle’s responsibilities when hacked funds move through USDC and its bridge. Regulatory commentary from the Federal Reserve and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency on nonbank access, along with how peers such as Ripple and Tether respond, can provide additional context on how competitive and tightly supervised this route into U.S. payment rails might become.

data.title: Analysts See AMD Poised To Capture More AI Data Center Demand
data.description:
• Analysts, including DA Davidson, have upgraded NasdaqGS:AMD to Buy following Intel's strong Q1 earnings and outlook.
  • The upgrades come as AI related CPU demand expands and Intel faces supply constraints in data center products.
  • Analysts argue that AMD's EPYC server CPUs are positioned to capture additional data center share tied to AI workloads.
  • The shift in analyst views frames AMD and Intel as the two primary CPU providers for AI focused data centers.

Advanced Micro Devices, traded as NasdaqGS:AMD, designs CPUs and GPUs that power PCs, gaming consoles and data centers. The fresh analyst attention centers on AMD's EPYC server chips, which are used in cloud and enterprise infrastructure that is increasingly configured for AI workloads. As AI training and inference rely more on CPU plus accelerator setups, the role of general purpose compute in data centers is receiving more focus from investors.

For you as an investor, the key question is how AI related demand for CPUs and accelerators could shape AMD's mix between PCs, gaming and data center over time. This recent analyst shift highlights AI centric data center exposure as a potential driver to watch, alongside product roadmaps, ecosystem support and how cloud providers allocate spend between Intel and AMD in future procurement cycles.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Advanced Micro Devices by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Advanced Micro Devices.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Advanced Micro Devices following this latest news.

The analyst upgrade for Advanced Micro Devices following Intel’s strong Q1 results signals a clear shift in how investors are thinking about AI related CPU demand. Intel’s report pointed to tight supply and strong AI server demand, which some analysts now read as evidence that general purpose CPUs are becoming more central in AI data centers alongside GPUs. That is important for you because it frames AMD’s EPYC server CPUs and Instinct accelerators as direct beneficiaries of a broader CPU upcycle, not just a single company story. The double effect here is useful to watch: sentiment around AI chips is lifting the whole group, and at the same time, supply constraints at one of the two main x86 vendors could channel incremental demand toward AMD if customers look to diversify suppliers.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Advanced Micro Devices Narrative

  • The upgrade supports the existing narrative that AMD is a key AI infrastructure player, with EPYC CPUs and data center GPUs positioned to benefit from stronger AI related workloads in cloud and enterprise environments.
  • It challenges the more cautious parts of the narrative that focus on execution and competitive pressure, as stronger CPU demand at Intel can be read as validation of the whole x86 AI server category rather than a clear win for AMD alone.
  • The immediate swing in analyst sentiment following Intel’s supply constraints may not be fully captured in longer term narrative models that focus on multi year revenue, margin and capital intensity assumptions.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Advanced Micro Devices to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Intel’s strong AI related CPU results and ongoing strength at Nvidia and Arm keep competitive pressure high in data center compute, which can affect AMD’s pricing power and share gains.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged at least 1 minor risk, including concerns around insider transactions, which some investors view as a signal to watch alongside rapid sentiment shifts.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow at around 31.76% per year, pointing to expectations that AI data center, client and gaming exposure can support meaningful profit expansion.
  • 🎁 Earnings grew by 159.8% over the past year, which underpins why investors are closely tracking AI related catalysts such as EPYC adoption and new accelerator ramps.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how AMD’s upcoming earnings on May 5 line up with these stronger AI CPU expectations, especially commentary on EPYC server demand, AI accelerator supply and any evidence that hyperscalers are shifting orders between Intel, Nvidia and AMD. Also watch whether references to “supply constrained” environments persist, because that can influence how much incremental share AMD can realistically capture in AI focused data centers. For you as an investor, the key is whether data center growth, margin trends and capital spending plans continue to support the richer expectations now embedded in analyst models.

  • Net income excluding extra items was US$0.8 million in Q1 2026, compared with US$4.2 million in Q4 2025 and trailing twelve month net income of US$12.0 million, so the latest quarter sits well below the recent annual run rate.
  • What stands out for a cautiously bullish view is that trailing twelve month EPS of US$0.14 and net income of US$12.0 million follow earlier loss making periods, even though the single quarter Q1 2026 EPS of roughly US$0.01 and net income of US$0.8 million highlight that profitability is still relatively thin.
    • Earlier quarters in the trailing set included losses, such as a trailing twelve month net loss of US$15.4 million in Q4 2024. In that context, the latest US$12.0 million profit marks a clear shift in the reported figures.

Approach 1: Farmers National Banc Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to earn over and above the return that shareholders require, then adds that value to the underlying book value per share. It is essentially asking how efficiently each dollar of equity is being used, after accounting for the cost of that equity.

For Farmers National Banc, the model starts with a Book Value of $12.95 per share and a Stable Book Value estimate of $14.27 per share, based on weighted future book value estimates from 3 analysts. Using a Stable EPS of $1.79 per share, sourced from the median return on equity from the past 5 years, and a Cost of Equity of $1.00 per share, the implied Excess Return is $0.79 per share. This aligns with an Average Return on Equity of 12.52%.

Combining these inputs, Simply Wall St's Excess Returns model arrives at an intrinsic value estimate of about $36.05 per share. Versus the current share price of around $14.04, the implied intrinsic discount of 61.1% suggests the shares are treated as materially undervalued by this framework.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Farmers National Banc Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is often the cleanest way to connect what you pay today with the earnings the business is currently generating. It tells you how many dollars investors are willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on what the market expects from a company and how risky those earnings appear. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually call for a lower multiple.

Farmers National Banc currently trades on a P/E of 14.52x. That is above both the Banks industry average P/E of 11.71x and the peer group average of 11.84x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 17.21x for Farmers National Banc, which reflects factors such as its earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers, because it adjusts for company specific features rather than treating all banks as identical.

Comparing the current P/E of 14.52x with the Fair Ratio of 17.21x indicates that the shares are pricing in a lower multiple than this framework would suggest.
